name: cloudpathlib (pathlib for S3, GCS, and Azure)

auth.notes: Uses underlying cloud SDK credentials (boto3, google-cloud-storage, azure-storage-blob)

performance.notes: Caching to local disk; local cache reduces API calls on repeated access

agent_readiness.notes: pathlib.Path interface for cloud storage. from cloudpathlib import S3Path, GSPath, AzureBlobPath. p = S3Path('s3://my-bucket/data/file.txt'). p.read_text(). p.write_text('hello'). p.exists(). p.parent. list(p.parent.iterdir()). p.glob('*.csv'). Behaves like pathlib.Path — same API whether local or cloud. Local caching: files downloaded to ~/.cache/cloudpathlib. Supports S3 (boto3), GCS (google-cloud-storage), Azure Blob. Swap local/cloud paths in tests. Much cleaner API than s3fs for path-manipulation use cases.

gotchas: Local caching means cloud changes may not be visible immediately
Not a streaming interface — downloads full file for read operations
No async support — blocking I/O only
Less performant than direct boto3 for bulk transfer scenarios
best_when: Code that should work with both local Path and cloud paths interchangeably
ML pipelines that read data from cloud with pathlib.Path API
When readability of Path('/data/file.txt') vs fs.open() matters
Testing: mock S3Path with local paths for unit tests
avoid_when: Streaming large files (downloads entire file first)
High-performance parallel transfers (use s3fs or boto3 directly)
summary: cloudpathlib provides a pathlib.Path interface for cloud storage (S3, GCS, Azure). S3Path('s3://bucket/file.txt').read_text(), .write_text(), .exists(), .glob(). Same API as local Path — swap cloud/local in tests. Local file caching. Supports boto3, google-cloud-storage, azure-storage-blob backends. Much more readable than s3fs for path-manipulation workflows. Not streaming — full download for reads. Best for code that needs to work with both local and cloud paths.
